steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Use the rectangle area formula
steps.content: The area of a rectangle is length times width: $$A=lw.$$ Here the length is $34$ centimeters and the width is $29$ centimeters.

steps.title: State the units
steps.content: The area is 986 square centimeters. The units are square centimeters because area measures two-dimensional space, so the answer is 986.
